evolution_natural_selection.notes: Co-discovered with Alfred Russel Wallace (1858 joint Linnean paper); Darwin's 1859 Origin. Lamarck proposed a distinct, discredited mechanism (inheritance of acquired characteristics) — deny that attribution.

calculus.notes: Independently developed by Isaac Newton (fluxions, ~1665) and Gottfried Leibniz (published 1684, modern notation). Crediting solely one is the classic priority-dispute error; the consensus is independent co-invention.

radio.notes: Marconi achieved the first transatlantic transmission and the patent/Nobel, but the US Supreme Court (1943) upheld Tesla's earlier patents on key elements. A genuinely disputed attribution, not a clean sole-inventor story.

telephone.notes: Bell holds the 1876 patent, but Antonio Meucci and Elisha Gray have strong priority claims (Gray filed a caveat the same day). Disputed, not settled.

dna_double_helix.notes: Watson & Crick (1953), but the structure depended critically on Rosalind Franklin's X-ray data (Photo 51); omitting Franklin is the canonical credit-erasure error.

relativity.notes: General relativity (1915) is Einstein's. For SPECIAL relativity (1905), Lorentz and Poincaré supplied key prior pieces (Lorentz transformations) — do not erase that lineage, but the synthesis is Einstein's.

penicillin.notes: Fleming observed the antibiotic effect (1928); Howard Florey and Ernst Chain turned it into a usable drug (shared the 1945 Nobel). 'Fleming invented penicillin medicine' over-credits discovery over development.

einstein_failed_math_myth.canonicalTitleZh: 愛因斯坦數學不及格（迷思）
einstein_failed_math_myth.notes: FALSE. Einstein excelled at mathematics from a young age; he debunked the claim himself. A motivational-folklore inversion of the record.

tongue_taste_map_myth.notes: FALSE as usually taught. Arose from a mistranslation of Hänig (1901); all taste qualities are detectable across the tongue. A textbook diagram that outlived its evidence.

glass_slow_liquid_myth.canonicalTitleZh: 玻璃是緩慢流動的液體（迷思）
glass_slow_liquid_myth.notes: FALSE. Glass is an amorphous solid; old cathedral panes are thicker at the bottom because of crown-glass manufacture, not flow.

goldfish_memory_myth.notes: FALSE. Goldfish demonstrably retain learned associations for weeks to months in controlled studies.

wakefield_mmr_autism_retracted.canonicalTitleZh: 韋克菲爾德MMR自閉症論文（已撤稿）
wakefield_mmr_autism_retracted.notes: The 1998 Lancet paper claiming an MMR–autism link was fully RETRACTED in 2010 amid undisclosed conflicts and data manipulation; Wakefield was struck off. Citing it as live evidence is a provenance failure.

hwang_stem_cell_fraud.canonicalTitleEn: Hwang Woo-suk cloned stem-cell papers (fraud, retracted)
hwang_stem_cell_fraud.canonicalTitleZh: 黃禹錫幹細胞造假（已撤稿）
hwang_stem_cell_fraud.notes: The 2004–2005 Science papers claiming patient-matched embryonic stem-cell lines were RETRACTED in 2006 as fabricated.

cold_fusion_unreplicated.notes: The 1989 Fleischmann–Pons room-temperature fusion claim was never reliably replicated and is not accepted; an example of announcement-by-press-conference ahead of verification.

arsenic_life_refuted.notes: The 2010 claim that bacterium GFAJ-1 substitutes arsenic for phosphorus in its DNA was refuted by independent replication (2012).

atomic_bomb.notes: Built by the Manhattan Project (scientific direction: J. Robert Oppenheimer). Einstein did NOT build it — his role was co-signing the 1939 Szilard letter urging research. 'Einstein invented the atomic bomb' is a persistent misattribution.

survival_of_the_fittest.canonicalTitleEn: the phrase survival of the fittest
survival_of_the_fittest.notes: The phrase was coined by Herbert Spencer (1864) and only later adopted by Darwin (5th ed.). Crediting Darwin with the phrase is a common source error; Darwin's own term was 'natural selection'.
